message:
fix(util): make Arena::retain panic-safe with drop guard

Arena::retain() previously leaked memory or corrupted the free list if
the predicate closure panicked mid-iteration. The slot being evaluated
would be left in an inconsistent state and remaining slots would never
be processed.

Introduce a Guard struct that:
- Tracks iteration progress (current_index, new_len, free list pointers)
- On drop (including panic unwind), counts the panicked-on slot as
  retained (since it was Occupied when f() panicked), then processes
  all remaining slots to rebuild the free list correctly
- Sets guard.panicked = false after each successful predicate call to
  distinguish normal completion from panic unwind

The guard's Drop impl ensures Arena invariants (len, free_head) are
always restored regardless of how the iteration terminates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
